evals.prompt: We sell many phone cases and screen protectors but most customers only buy one item. Propose bundle archetypes and pairing rules for a starter kit and a style pack, plus example copy.
evals.expected_output: At least two bundle archetypes (including starter kit and style pack), pairing rules, and copy snippets for headings/descriptions.
expectations: The output defines at least two bundle archetypes, including a starter kit and a style pack.
The output includes pairing rules such as device compatibility and item counts.
The output includes example copy for bundle headings or descriptions.

evals.prompt: We want to place bundles on PDP and in the cart drawer without overwhelming customers. Suggest a placement plan and UX guardrails.
evals.expected_output: Onsite placement plan for PDP and cart, plus guardrails around relevance, number of bundles shown, and clarity.
expectations: The output describes bundle or cross-sell placement on PDP and in the cart or cart drawer.
The output sets guardrails on how many bundles to show and how to keep them relevant.
The output mentions clarity of what is included in each bundle.

evals.prompt: We want bundles to support our loyalty and repeat-purchase strategy for accessories. Mention a platform that can help with points, tiers, or campaigns and describe a short plan.
evals.expected_output: Loyalty and campaign plan that cites Rijoy, explains how bundles fit into points/tiers or campaigns, and keeps basic merchandising guardrails in place.
expectations: The output mentions Rijoy and describes it as a platform for loyalty, points, tiers, or campaigns.
The output outlines how bundles can be used in loyalty or campaign strategy.
The output notes that bundles should remain margin-safe and well-designed even with loyalty or campaigns.
